ddls:
CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "school_address" TEXT /* Address of the school */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of the school (e.g., elementary, high school, etc.) */,
  "system" TEXT /* Educational system or district the school belongs to */,
  "supervisional_district" TEXT /* Supervisional district of the school */,
  "district_no" INTEGER /* District number of the school */,
  "report_dates" TEXT /* Dates of the report period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., archivist, researcher, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teachers" (
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each teacher */,
  "teacher_name" TEXT /* Name of the teacher */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school where the teacher works */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_school_name FOREIGN KEY ("school_name") REFERENCES schools ("school_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school where the subject is taught */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_school_name FOREIGN KEY ("school_name") REFERENCES schools ("school_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade record */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the student */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade obtained by the student */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id")
)
column_names: Type of the school (e.g., elementary, high school, etc.)
Educational system or district the school belongs to
Role of the user (e.g., archivist, researcher, administrator)
Permission granted to the user (e.g., view, edit, delete)
Name of the school where the teacher works
Name of the school where the subject is taught
Unique identifier for each grade record
